## Intel Valuation Falls to Levels Not Seen Even During the Dot-Com Bust

INTC at sub-dot-com valuations is either a deep-value inflection signal or a value trap — the critical distinction is whether the damage is cyclical (semiconductor demand) or structural (permanent share loss to AMD and TSMC-backed fabless players), and the headline does not resolve that question.

- INTC is trading at a valuation level not reached even at the trough of the dot-com era, per the headline.
- The framing implies structural — not merely cyclical — impairment of the investment case.
- No specific new catalyst is cited; this appears to be a valuation commentary piece.
- Competitive displacement from AMD and the fabless model is the implicit structural backdrop.
